channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-506] FC15 patchless client support.
item.description: This ticket want to trace FC15 support progress.

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Landed for 2.3. Please open new JIRA tickets to track any further enhancements or bugfixes in this area.

Hi, Iurii,

I am afraid haven't enough time to do that in near future.

Regards,
YangSheng
Patch http://review.whamcloud.com/2336 needs reviewers assigned.

(update - repeat from 6th of March)
Hi Yang Sheng.

We require this fix for the 1.8 also. (LU-1062)

Could you please merge your fix to the 1.8 branch?

If you are too busy let me know and I'll do this by myself.

Thank you,
Iurii Golovach
I pushed http://review.whamcloud.com/2336 for master to fix the memset() autoconf bug.

Your approach in http://review.whamcloud.com/1330 of adding new code, rather than compiler directives like Ned used in LU-251, means that you risk introducing new failures:

/g/g0/morrone/src/lustre-bgq/build/conftest.c: In function 'main':
/g/g0/morrone/src/lustre-bgq/build/conftest.c:118: error: implicit declaration of function 'memset'
/g/g0/morrone/src/lustre-bgq/build/conftest.c:118: warning: incompatible implicit declaration of built-in function 'memset'

The above is from the "checking fs_struct use path structure" test, which really should not be doing a memset in the first place.

I think you really need to have a code comment in the test as well any time you are doing something just to work around a compiler warning. Otherwise the extra code just obfuscates the purpose of the autoconf test.

The 

"__attribute__ ((unused))"

compiler directive seems pretty self-explanatory to me in contrast.

Hi Yang Sheng.

We require this fix for the 1.8 also. (LU-1062)

Could you please merge your fix to the 1.8 branch?

If you are too busy let me know and I'll do this by myself.

Thank you,
Iurii Golovach

http://review.whamcloud.com/1845 vfsmount.mnt_count doesn't use atomic_t

it doesn't change mnt_count access in llite/llite_lib.c, is that normal or just missed? Don't we need to use mnt_get_cnt() there anyway for consistency?

for the record, other already landed patches link:

http://review.whamcloud.com/1800 blkdev_get_by_dev() used instead of open_by_devnum()
http://review.whamcloud.com/1739 tcp_sendpage() uses struct sock
http://review.whamcloud.com/1738 unplug_fn removed since 2.6.39
http://review.whamcloud.com/1332 ctl_name & strategy removed from ctl_table
http://review.whamcloud.com/1788 update shrinker to use shrink_control callback
http://review.whamcloud.com/1789 move some headers to include/generated

The FC15 kernel-2.6.38.8-35 patchless client support patches already submit to gerrit:

http://review.whamcloud.com/1330 fix GCC 'set-but-unused' warnings
http://review.whamcloud.com/1843 generic_permission() need 4 arguments.
http://review.whamcloud.com/1844 super_operations add evict_inode method
http://review.whamcloud.com/1845 vfsmount.mnt_count doesn't use atomic_t
http://review.whamcloud.com/1862 file_operations relate changes
http://review.whamcloud.com/1863 inode_setattr() replaced by simple_setattr()
http://review.whamcloud.com/1864 small changes
http://review.whamcloud.com/1865 Dcache RCU-walk changes.

Please note that have dependence exist between some patches. So better apply them as order.
